summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact.

bullets.text: Lead strategic learning initiatives end to end—needs assessment, design, pilot, and launch—with {cross|cross-functional} stakeholders and 8+ vendors
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify {prog|learning} gaps and design 3 new educational programs
Develop {prog|learning materials}—guides and workflows—for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate {prog|training} webinars for 600+ participants, assessing feedback to improve solutions and achieving a 78% program enrollment rate
Provided consultations on strategic plans and internal {prog|training} for managers on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, translating {data|data} into actionable reports demonstrating impact on leadership development
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights and recommending actions to the C-suite for employee engagement
Elevated employee experience through projects focused on engagement, rewards & recognition, survey development, and {prog|L&D}
Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership {prog|workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 global locations
Optimized onboarding processes across global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Merger & Acquisition project, migrating and merging employee records
Assisted in employee relations cases, ensuring compliance with local labor laws and fostering a positive work environment
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized campus recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite executives at top universities to attract talent

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Nike — Lead, Learning & Development Advisor (Beaverton, Portland-local). Location → Portland, OR (local). Summary → Learning & Development umbrella, data-driven approaches. InGenius FIRST: led with 'lead strategic learning initiatives end to end—needs assessment, design, pilot, launch—with cross-functional stakeholders and 8+ vendors' to mirror JD's 'create design briefs / curate, build, test and pilot / launch and sustain / cross-functional collaboration / vendor management.' Webinar bullet reworded to add 'assessing feedback to improve solutions' (JD: 'assess qualitative and quantitative feedback to improve learning experiences vs intended outcomes'). NextGen reframed around manager/leadership development (JD: 'global L&D strategy for people-manager development'). Vestas keeps Inclusive Leadership workshop scaled to 23 locations (manager-development + global signal). Skills → Learning Systems adds Leadership/Manager Development; Data&PM adds Vendor & Budget Management (JD: 'managing budgets, optimizing spend, vendor management'). STRETCH NOTE: JD asks 5-7 yrs adult-learning solution development + global manager-development strategy ownership; Jamie has ~3 yrs and strong end-to-end L&D program delivery + leadership-development exposure, but not multi-year global-strategy ownership. Tailored honestly to real program-leadership strengths — did not invent strategy-ownership scope. Cover acknowledges the level candidly while making the transferable case.
